      folder_id INTEGER,
      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id),
      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
    )
  `);

  const bookmarkColumns = db
    .prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)")
    .all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)",
    );
  }

  if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)",
    );
  }

  const folderColumns = db
    .prepare("PRAGMA table_info(folders)")
    .all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!folderColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE folders ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)",
    );
  }
}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:133:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_68","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' docs/product/api.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Authentication

Users are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are
stored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer
token after authentication.

### `POST /auth/register`

Creates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.

### `POST /auth/login`

Authenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "token": "<jwt>" }
```

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one
of that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.

All bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered
by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned
by the authenticated user.

All folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by
`id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the
authenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

import { db } from "./db";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findBookmark(id: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required and must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.url.trim(), body.title.trim(), description, now, now);

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid));
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  let url = existing.url;
  let title = existing.title;
  let description = existing.description;

  if (body.url !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be valid" });
      return;
    }

    url = body.url.trim();
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    title = body.title.trim();
  }

  if (body.description !== undefined) {
    description = typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  }
  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, updatedAt, id);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` is optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of all bookmarks ordered by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, and/or `description` for an existing bookmark and
refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

# T2 CRUD Bookmarks

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The API exposes full CRUD operations for bookmarks and persists bookmark data in
SQLite `data.db`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `docs/product/api.md`
- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark with required `url` and `title`.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns an array of bookmarks.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns a bookmark or HTTP `404`.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates a bookmark or returns HTTP `404`.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes a bookmark with HTTP `204` or returns HTTP
  `404`.
- Missing or empty required bookmark fields return HTTP `400`.
- `GET /health` continues to return HTTP `200`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, and delete bookmark rows.
- Queries: list all bookmarks and get a bookmark by id.
- API: `/bookmarks` collection and `/bookmarks/:id` item routes.
- Tables: `bookmarks`.
- Domain rules: `url` and `title` are required for creation; `title` must be
  non-empty.
- UI surfaces: none.

# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl

check_functional() {
  local task="$1"
  local outdir="$2"
  local project_dir="$3"

  local results=()
  local server_pid=""
  local port=3000
  local base_url="http://localhost:$port"

  # Try to start the server
  (cd "$project_dir" && npm run dev > "$outdir/server.log" 2>&1) &
  server_pid=$!
  sleep 3

  # Check if server is alive
  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  Server failed to start"
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 10s)
  local ready=false
  for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$base_url/health" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|404\|500"; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done

  if [ "$ready" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  Server not responding on port $port"
    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Run task-specific checks
  case "$task" in
    T1-project-setup)
      run_check results "health_status" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      run_check results "health_body" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200 --body-contains '"status"'
      ;;

    T2-crud-bookmarks)
      # Create
      run_check results "create_bookmark" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test Bookmark"}'
      # List
      run_check results "list_bookmarks" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      # Get single
      run_check results "get_bookmark" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200
      # Update
      run_check results "update_bookmark" "PUT" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200 \
        --data '{"title":"Updated Title"}'
      # Delete
      run_check results "delete_bookmark" "DELETE" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 204
      # Validation
      run_check results "missing_title" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
      run_check results "missing_url" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"title":"No URL"}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "health_regression" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      ;;

    T3-folder-support)
      # Folder CRUD
      run_check results "create_folder" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 201 \
        --data '{"name":"Reading List"}'
      run_check results "list_folders" "GET" "$base_url/folders" 200
      run_check results "get_folder" "GET" "$base_url/folders/1" 200
      # Bookmark with folder
      run_check results "bookmark_with_folder" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Foldered","folder_id":1}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "bookmark_regression" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      ;;

    T4-authentication)
      # Register
      run_check results "register" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 201 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Duplicate
      run_check results "duplicate_email" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 409 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      run_check results "login" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 200 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Wrong password
      run_check results "wrong_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 401 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"wrongpass"}'
      # Protected (no auth)
      run_check results "protected_no_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      # Protected (with auth)
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "protected_with_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        # Create with auth
        run_check results "create_with_auth" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Authed Bookmark"}'
      fi
      ;;

    T5-bug-fix)
      # Login first
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      # If no existing user, register first
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_title_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":""}'
        run_check results "empty_url_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"","title":"Test"}'
        run_check results "valid_still_works" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Valid"}'
      fi
      # Folder empty name
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_folder_name" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"name":""}'
      fi
      ;;

    T6-pagination)
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        # Check paginated response shape
        local response
        response=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" 2>/dev/null)

        # Has expected fields
        run_check_json results "has_data_field" "$response" '.data'
        run_check_json results "has_page_field" "$response" '.page'
        run_check_json results "has_limit_field" "$response" '.limit'
        run_check_json results "has_total_field" "$response" '.total'
        run_check_json results "data_is_array" "$response" '.data | type == "array"'

        # Custom pagination
        run_check results "custom_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Invalid
        run_check results "invalid_page" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=0" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        run_check results "invalid_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Auth still required
        run_check results "still_protected" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # Stop server
  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true

  # Write results
  write_functional_json "$outdir/functional.json" "${results[@]}"
}

# Helper: run a single endpoint check
run_check() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local method="$1"; shift
  local url="$1"; shift
  local expected_status="$1"; shift

  local extra_args=()
  local body_contains=""
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --header) extra_args+=(-H "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --body-contains) body_contains="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      *) shift ;;
    esac
  done

  local response_code response_body
  response_body=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X "$method" "${extra_args[@]}" "$url" 2>/dev/null)
  response_code=$(echo "$response_body" | tail -1)
  response_body=$(echo "$response_body" | sed '$d')

  local pass=false
  if [ "$response_code" = "$expected_status" ]; then
    pass=true
  fi

  # Optional body check
  if [ -n "$body_contains" ] && [ "$pass" = "true" ]; then
    if ! echo "$response_body" | grep -q "$body_contains"; then
      pass=false
    fi
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass,\"expected\":$expected_status,\"actual\":$response_code}")
}

# Helper: check JSON response field
run_check_json() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local json="$1"; shift
  local jq_expr="$1"; shift

  local pass=false
  if echo "$json" | jq -e "$jq_expr" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    pass=true
  fi

  arr+=("{\"name\":\"$name\",\"pass\":$pass}")
}

# Helper: write results JSON
write_functional_json() {
  local outfile="$1"; shift
  local checks=("$@")

import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid)));
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const folders = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    db.prepare("UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL WHERE folder_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
    );
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ?").run(folderId);
  });

  deleteFolder(id);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : undefined;
}

function findBookmark(id: number): Bookmark | undefined {

}

function folderExists(id: number): boolean {
  const row = db.prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ?").get(id);

}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:70:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_37","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,160p' src/db.ts"","aggregated_output":"import Database from "better-sqlite3";

export const db = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

export function initializeDatabase(): void {
  db.exec(`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      url TEXT NOT NULL,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      folder_id INTEGER,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id)
    )
  `);

  const columns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!columns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
    db.exec("ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)");
  }
}

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;
import { Router } from "express";
import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid)));
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const folders = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    db.prepare("UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL WHERE folder_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
    );
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ?").run(folderId);
  });

  deleteFolder(id);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;
import Database from "better-sqlite3";

export const db = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

export function initializeDatabase(): void {
  db.exec(`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      url TEXT NOT NULL,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      folder_id INTEGER,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id)
    )
  `);

  const columns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!columns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)",
    );
  }
}
// Bookmark Manager API — entrypoint
// See PRODUCT_SPEC.md for full requirements
// The agent will build this out across tasks T1-T6

import express from "express";
import bookmarksRouter from "./bookmarks";
import { initializeDatabase } from "./db";
import foldersRouter from "./folders";

const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.use(express.json());

initializeDatabase();

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.use("/bookmarks", bookmarksRouter);
app.use("/folders", foldersRouter);

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:26:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_12","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl

check_functional() {
  local task="$1"
  local outdir="$2"
  local project_dir="$3"

  local results=()
  local server_pid=""
  local port=3000
  local base_url="http://localhost:$port"

  # Try to start the server
  (cd "$project_dir" && npm run dev > "$outdir/server.log" 2>&1) &
  server_pid=$!
  sleep 3

  # Check if server is alive
  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "  Server failed to start"
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 10s)
  local ready=false
  for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "$base_url/health" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "200\|404\|500"; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done

  if [ "$ready" = "false" ]; then
    echo "  Server not responding on port $port"
    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
    echo '{"checks":[],"server_started":false}' > "$outdir/functional.json"
    return 1
  fi

  # Run task-specific checks
  case "$task" in
    T1-project-setup)
      run_check results "health_status" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      run_check results "health_body" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200 --body-contains '"status"'
      ;;

    T2-crud-bookmarks)
      # Create
      run_check results "create_bookmark" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test Bookmark"}'
      # List
      run_check results "list_bookmarks" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      # Get single
      run_check results "get_bookmark" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200
      # Update
      run_check results "update_bookmark" "PUT" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 200 \
        --data '{"title":"Updated Title"}'
      # Delete
      run_check results "delete_bookmark" "DELETE" "$base_url/bookmarks/1" 204
      # Validation
      run_check results "missing_title" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com"}'
      run_check results "missing_url" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
        --data '{"title":"No URL"}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "health_regression" "GET" "$base_url/health" 200
      ;;

    T3-folder-support)
      # Folder CRUD
      run_check results "create_folder" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 201 \
        --data '{"name":"Reading List"}'
      run_check results "list_folders" "GET" "$base_url/folders" 200
      run_check results "get_folder" "GET" "$base_url/folders/1" 200
      # Bookmark with folder
      run_check results "bookmark_with_folder" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
        --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Foldered","folder_id":1}'
      # Regression
      run_check results "bookmark_regression" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200
      ;;

    T4-authentication)
      # Register
      run_check results "register" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 201 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Duplicate
      run_check results "duplicate_email" "POST" "$base_url/auth/register" 409 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      run_check results "login" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 200 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}'
      # Wrong password
      run_check results "wrong_password" "POST" "$base_url/auth/login" 401 \
        --data '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"wrongpass"}'
      # Protected (no auth)
      run_check results "protected_no_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      # Protected (with auth)
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "protected_with_auth" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        # Create with auth
        run_check results "create_with_auth" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Authed Bookmark"}'
      fi
      ;;

    T5-bug-fix)
      # Login first
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      # If no existing user, register first
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_title_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":""}'
        run_check results "empty_url_400" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"","title":"Test"}'
        run_check results "valid_still_works" "POST" "$base_url/bookmarks" 201 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"url":"https://example.com","title":"Valid"}'
      fi
      # Folder empty name
      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        run_check results "empty_folder_name" "POST" "$base_url/folders" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token" \
          --data '{"name":""}'
      fi
      ;;

    T6-pagination)
      # Login
      local token
      token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
        -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      if [ -z "$token" ]; then
        curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/register" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
        token=$(curl -s -X POST "$base_url/auth/login" \
          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
          -d '{"email":"bench@test.com","password":"benchmark123"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')
      fi

      if [ -n "$token" ]; then
        # Check paginated response shape
        local response
        response=$(curl -s "$base_url/bookmarks" -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" 2>/dev/null)

        # Has expected fields
        run_check_json results "has_data_field" "$response" '.data'
        run_check_json results "has_page_field" "$response" '.page'
        run_check_json results "has_limit_field" "$response" '.limit'
        run_check_json results "has_total_field" "$response" '.total'
        run_check_json results "data_is_array" "$response" '.data | type == "array"'

        # Custom pagination
        run_check results "custom_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5" 200 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Invalid
        run_check results "invalid_page" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?page=0" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"
        run_check results "invalid_limit" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200" 400 \
          --header "Authorization: Bearer $token"

        # Auth still required
        run_check results "still_protected" "GET" "$base_url/bookmarks" 401
      fi
      ;;
  esac

  # Stop server
  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true
  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true

  # Write results
  write_functional_json "$outdir/functional.json" "${results[@]}"
}

# Helper: run a single endpoint check
run_check() {
  local -n arr=$1; shift
  local name="$1"; shift
  local method="$1"; shift
  local url="$1"; shift
  local expected_status="$1"; shift

  local extra_args=()
  local body_contains=""
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case $1 in
      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --header) extra_args+=(-H "$2"); shift 2 ;;
      --body-contains) body_contains="$2"; shift 2 ;;
      *) shift ;;
    esac
  done

  local response_code response_body

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:72:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_38","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { db } from "./db";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findBookmark(id: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required and must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.url.trim(), body.title.trim(), description, now, now);

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid));
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  if (
    body.url !== undefined &&
    (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim()))
  ) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined && !isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  const url = body.url === undefined ? existing.url : body.url.trim();
  const title = body.title === undefined ? existing.title : body.title.trim();
  const description =
    body.description === undefined
      ? existing.description
      : typeof body.description === "string"
        ? body.description
        : null;
  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, updatedAt, id);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      url TEXT NOT NULL,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    )
  `);

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:76:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_40","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' docs/product/api.md && sed -n '1,220p' docs/stories/T2-crud-bookmarks.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` is optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of all bookmarks ordered by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, and/or `description` for an existing bookmark and
refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.
# T2 CRUD Bookmarks

## Status

in_progress

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The API exposes full CRUD operations for bookmarks and persists bookmark data in
SQLite `data.db`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `docs/product/api.md`
- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /bookmarks` creates a bookmark with required `url` and `title`.
- `GET /bookmarks` returns an array of bookmarks.
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` returns a bookmark or HTTP `404`.
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` updates a bookmark or returns HTTP `404`.
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` deletes a bookmark with HTTP `204` or returns HTTP
  `404`.
- Missing or empty required bookmark fields return HTTP `400`.
- `GET /health` continues to return HTTP `200`.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, and delete bookmark rows.
- Queries: list all bookmarks and get a bookmark by id.
- API: `/bookmarks` collection and `/bookmarks/:id` item routes.
- Tables: `bookmarks`.
- Domain rules: `url` and `title` are required for creation; `title` must be
  non-empty.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build succeeds. |
| Integration | Curl checks cover all CRUD endpoints, validation failures, not found, and health regression. |
| E2E | Not applicable for API-only story. |
| Platform | SQLite `data.db` persists bookmark rows locally. |
| Release | Not applicable for benchmark task. |

## Harness Delta

No harness process changes were needed.

# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Authentication

Users are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are
stored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer
token after authentication.

### `POST /auth/register`

Creates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.

### `POST /auth/login`

Authenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "token": "<jwt>" }
```

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one
of that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.

All bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing, empty, whitespace-only,
or when `url` is not a valid URL.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered
by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is provided as an empty or
whitespace-only string, or when `url` is not a valid URL.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned
by the authenticated user.

All folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing, empty, or whitespace-only.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by
`id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the
authenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` when provided and refreshes `updated_at` when the
name changes.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is provided as an empty or whitespace-only
string.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

  harness story update --id <id> [flags]

  --evidence <text>  Validation evidence
  --unit <0|1>       Unit proof status
  --integration <0|1>

  --platform <0|1>

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:16:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_7","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_5","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

import express from "express";
import authRouter from "./auth";
import bookmarksRouter from "./bookmarks";
import { initializeDatabase } from "./db";
import foldersRouter from "./folders";

const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.use(express.json());

initializeDatabase();

app.get("/health", (_req, res) => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: "ok" });
});

app.use("/auth", authRouter);
app.use("/bookmarks", bookmarksRouter);
app.use("/folders", foldersRouter);

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T5-bug-fix/events.jsonl:34:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_16","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,280p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { requireAuth } from "./auth";
import { db } from "./db";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

router.use(requireAuth);

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return undefined;
  }

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value === "number") {
    return Number.isInteger(value) && value > 0 ? value : undefined;
  }

  if (typeof value === "string" && /^\d+$/.test(value)) {
    const id = Number(value);
    return id > 0 ? id : undefined;
  }

  return undefined;
}

function findBookmark(id: number, userId: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`,
    )
    .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

function folderExists(id: number, userId: number): boolean {
  const row = db
    .prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
    .get(id, userId);
  return row !== undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required and must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  const folderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

  if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  if (typeof folderId === "number" && !folderExists(folderId, userId)) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(
      body.url.trim(),
      body.title.trim(),
      description,
      folderId ?? null,
      userId,
      now,
      now,
    );

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId);
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(userId) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  let url = existing.url;
  let title = existing.title;
  let description = existing.description;
  let folderId = existing.folder_id;

  if (body.url !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be valid" });
      return;
    }

    url = body.url.trim();
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    title = body.title.trim();
  }

  if (body.description !== undefined) {
    description = typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  }

  if (body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    const parsedFolderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

    if (parsedFolderId === undefined) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    if (
      typeof parsedFolderId === "number" &&
      !folderExists(parsedFolderId, userId)
    ) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    folderId = parsedFolderId;
  }

  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db
          .prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
          .run(id, userId);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

import { requireAuth } from "./auth";
import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

router.use(requireAuth);

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number, userId: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`)
    .get(id, userId) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), userId, now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId));
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const folders = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(userId) as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id, userId) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number, ownerId: number) => {
    db.prepare(
      "UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ? WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?",
    ).run(new Date().toISOString(), folderId, ownerId);
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
      ownerId,
    );
  });

  deleteFolder(id, userId);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Authentication

Users are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are
stored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer
token after authentication.

### `POST /auth/register`

Creates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.

### `POST /auth/login`

Authenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "token": "<jwt>" }
```

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one
of that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.

All bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered
by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned
by the authenticated user.

All folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by
`id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the
authenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.
