benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T3-folder-support/events.jsonl:65:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_34","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"sed -n '1,280p' src/folders.ts\"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from \"express\";\nimport { db } from \"./db\";\n\ntype Folder = {\n  id: number;\n  name: string;\n  created_at: string;\n  updated_at: string;\n};\n\ntype Bookmark = {\n  id: number;\n  url: string;\n  title: string;\n  description: string | null;\n  folder_id: number | null;\n  created_at: string;\n  updated_at: string;\n};\n\ntype FolderBody = {\n  name?: unknown;\n};\n\nconst router = Router();\n\nconst folderSelect = \"id, name, created_at, updated_at\";\nconst bookmarkSelect =\n  \"id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at\";\n\nfunction isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {\n  return typeof value === \"string\" && value.trim().length > 0;\n}\n\nfunction parseId(value: string): number | null {\n  const id = Number(value);\n  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;\n}\n\nfunction findFolder(id: number): Folder | undefined {\n  return db\n    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ?`)\n    .get(id) as Folder | undefined;\n}\n\nrouter.post(\"/\", (req, res) => {\n  const body = req.body as FolderBody;\n\n  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {\n    res.status(400).json({ error: \"name is required\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const now = new Date().toISOString();\n  const result = db\n    .prepare(\n      `\n        INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)\n        VALUES (?, ?, ?)\n      `,\n    )\n    .run(body.name.trim(), now, now);\n\n  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid)));\n});\n\nrouter.get(\"/\", (_req, res) => {\n  const folders = db\n    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders ORDER BY id ASC`)\n    .all() as Folder[];\n\n  res.status(200).json(folders);\n});\n\nrouter.get(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);\n\n  if (!folder || id === null) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const bookmarks = db\n    .prepare(\n      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,\n    )\n    .all(id) as Bookmark[];\n\n  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });\n});\n\nrouter.put(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);\n\n  if (!existing || id === null) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const body = req.body as FolderBody;\n\n  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {\n    res.status(400).json({ error: \"name must be non-empty\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  db.prepare(\n    `\n      UPDATE folders\n      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ?\n    `,\n  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id);\n\n  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id));\n});\n\nrouter.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);\n\n  if (!existing || id === null) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"folder not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {\n    db.prepare(\"UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL WHERE folder_id = ?\").run(\n      folderId,\n    );\n    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ?\").run(folderId);\n  });\n\n  deleteFolder(id);\n  res.status(204).send();\n});\n\nexport default router;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.\n\n## Functional Requirements\n\n### Core Entities\n\n- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps\n- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks\n- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n#### Health\n- `GET /health` → `{ \"status\": \"ok\" }`\n\n#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)\n- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)\n- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)\n- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark\n- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark\n- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark\n\n#### Folders\n- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)\n- `GET /folders` — List all folders\n- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks\n- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name\n- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)\n\n#### Authentication\n- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)\n- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token\n- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented\n\n### Validation Rules\n\n- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL\n- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string\n- Folder `name` must be non-empty string\n- Email must be valid format\n- Password must be >= 8 characters\n\n### Pagination (after T6)\n\n- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`\n- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`\n- Default: page=1, limit=20\n- Max limit: 100\n\n## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)\n\n- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+\n- **Language**: TypeScript\n- **Framework**: Express\n- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3\n- **Testing**: Vitest\n- **Port**: 3000\n\n## Non-Functional Requirements\n\n- All responses are JSON\n- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)\n- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)\n- JWT tokens for session management\n- Database file: `data.db` (application data)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

| Check | Method | Pass Criteria |\n|---|-------|--------|---------------|\n| 1 | Create bookmark | `POST /bookmarks {\"url\":\"https://example.com\",\"title\":\"Test\"}` | Status 201 |\n| 2 | List bookmarks | `GET /bookmarks` | Status 200 + array |\n| 3 | Get single | `GET /bookmarks/1` | Status 200 |\n| 4 | Update bookmark | `PUT /bookmarks/1 {\"title\":\"Updated\"}` | Status 200 |\n| 5 | Delete bookmark | `DELETE /bookmarks/1` | Status 204 |\n| 6 | Missing title | `POST /bookmarks {\"url\":\"https://example.com\"}` | Status 400 |\n| 7 | Missing url | `POST /bookmarks {\"title\":\"No URL\"}` | Status 400 |\n| 8 | Not found | `GET /bookmarks/9999` | Status 404 |\n| 9 | Health regression | `GET /health` | Status 200 |\n\n## Harness Compliance Checks\n\n| # | Check | Query |\n|---|-------|-------|\n| 1 | Intake recorded | `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intake` increased |\n| 2 | Risk lane = normal | Latest intake `risk_lane` = \"normal\" |\n| 3 | Story created | `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM story` > 0 |\n| 4 | Trace recorded | Latest trace exists |\n| 5 | Friction captured | Latest trace `harness_friction` is not null |\n\n## Quality Indicators\n\n- Trace `actions_taken` mentions creating table/schema\n- Trace `files_changed` lists multiple files\n- Story has meaningful title (> 5 chars)\n#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# benchmark/lib/check-functional.sh — API endpoint tests via curl\n\ncheck_functional() {\n  local task=\"$1\"\n  local outdir=\"$2\"\n  local project_dir=\"$3\"\n\n  local results=()\n  local server_pid=\"\"\n  local port=3000\n  local base_url=\"http://localhost:$port\"\n\n  # Try to start the server\n  (cd \"$project_dir\" && npm run dev > \"$outdir/server.log\" 2>&1) &\n  server_pid=$!\n  sleep 3\n\n  # Check if server is alive\n  if ! kill -0 $server_pid 2>/dev/null; then\n    echo \"  Server failed to start\"\n    echo '{\"checks\":[],\"server_started\":false}' > \"$outdir/functional.json\"\n    return 1\n  fi\n\n  # Wait for port to be ready (up to 10s)\n  local ready=false\n  for i in $(seq 1 10); do\n    if curl -s -o /dev/null -w \"%{http_code}\" \"$base_url/health\" 2>/dev/null | grep -q \"200\\|404\\|500\"; then\n      ready=true\n      break\n    fi\n    sleep 1\n  done\n\n  if [ \"$ready\" = \"false\" ]; then\n    echo \"  Server not responding on port $port\"\n    kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true\n    echo '{\"checks\":[],\"server_started\":false}' > \"$outdir/functional.json\"\n    return 1\n  fi\n\n  # Run task-specific checks\n  case \"$task\" in\n    T1-project-setup)\n      run_check results \"health_status\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/health\" 200\n      run_check results \"health_body\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/health\" 200 --body-contains '\"status\"'\n      ;;\n\n    T2-crud-bookmarks)\n      # Create\n      run_check results \"create_bookmark\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 201 \\\n        --data '{\"url\":\"https://example.com\",\"title\":\"Test Bookmark\"}'\n      # List\n      run_check results \"list_bookmarks\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 200\n      # Get single\n      run_check results \"get_bookmark\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks/1\" 200\n      # Update\n      run_check results \"update_bookmark\" \"PUT\" \"$base_url/bookmarks/1\" 200 \\\n        --data '{\"title\":\"Updated Title\"}'\n      # Delete\n      run_check results \"delete_bookmark\" \"DELETE\" \"$base_url/bookmarks/1\" 204\n      # Validation\n      run_check results \"missing_title\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 400 \\\n        --data '{\"url\":\"https://example.com\"}'\n      run_check results \"missing_url\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 400 \\\n        --data '{\"title\":\"No URL\"}'\n      # Regression\n      run_check results \"health_regression\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/health\" 200\n      ;;\n\n    T3-folder-support)\n      # Folder CRUD\n      run_check results \"create_folder\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/folders\" 201 \\\n        --data '{\"name\":\"Reading List\"}'\n      run_check results \"list_folders\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/folders\" 200\n      run_check results \"get_folder\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/folders/1\" 200\n      # Bookmark with folder\n      run_check results \"bookmark_with_folder\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 201 \\\n        --data '{\"url\":\"https://example.com\",\"title\":\"Foldered\",\"folder_id\":1}'\n      # Regression\n      run_check results \"bookmark_regression\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 200\n      ;;\n\n    T4-authentication)\n      # Register\n      run_check results \"register\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/auth/register\" 201 \\\n        --data '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}'\n      # Duplicate\n      run_check results \"duplicate_email\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/auth/register\" 409 \\\n        --data '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}'\n      # Login\n      local token\n      token=$(curl -s -X POST \"$base_url/auth/login\" \\\n        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n        -d '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')\n      run_check results \"login\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/auth/login\" 200 \\\n        --data '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}'\n      # Wrong password\n      run_check results \"wrong_password\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/auth/login\" 401 \\\n        --data '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"wrongpass\"}'\n      # Protected (no auth)\n      run_check results \"protected_no_auth\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 401\n      # Protected (with auth)\n      if [ -n \"$token\" ]; then\n        run_check results \"protected_with_auth\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 200 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\"\n        # Create with auth\n        run_check results \"create_with_auth\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 201 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\" \\\n          --data '{\"url\":\"https://example.com\",\"title\":\"Authed Bookmark\"}'\n      fi\n      ;;\n\n    T5-bug-fix)\n      # Login first\n      local token\n      token=$(curl -s -X POST \"$base_url/auth/login\" \\\n        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n        -d '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')\n      # Folder empty name\n      if [ -n \"$token\" ]; then\n        run_check results \"empty_folder_name\" \"POST\" \"$base_url/folders\" 400 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\" \\\n          --data '{\"name\":\"\"}'\n      fi\n      ;;\n\n    T6-pagination)\n      # Login\n      local token\n      token=$(curl -s -X POST \"$base_url/auth/login\" \\\n        -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n        -d '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')\n      if [ -z \"$token\" ]; then\n        curl -s -X POST \"$base_url/auth/register\" \\\n          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n          -d '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}' > /dev/null 2>&1\n        token=$(curl -s -X POST \"$base_url/auth/login\" \\\n          -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\\n          -d '{\"email\":\"bench@test.com\",\"password\":\"benchmark123\"}' 2>/dev/null | jq -r '.token // empty')\n      fi\n\n      if [ -n \"$token\" ]; then\n        # Check paginated response shape\n        local response\n        response=$(curl -s \"$base_url/bookmarks\" -H \"Authorization: Bearer $token\" 2>/dev/null)\n\n        # Has expected fields\n        run_check_json results \"has_data_field\" \"$response\" '.data'\n        run_check_json results \"has_page_field\" \"$response\" '.page'\n        run_check_json results \"has_limit_field\" \"$response\" '.limit'\n        run_check_json results \"has_total_field\" \"$response\" '.total'\n        run_check_json results \"data_is_array\" \"$response\" '.data | type == \"array\"'\n\n        # Custom pagination\n        run_check results \"custom_limit\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks?page=1&limit=5\" 200 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\"\n\n        # Invalid\n        run_check results \"invalid_page\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks?page=0\" 400 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\"\n        run_check results \"invalid_limit\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks?limit=200\" 400 \\\n          --header \"Authorization: Bearer $token\"\n\n        # Auth still required\n        run_check results \"still_protected\" \"GET\" \"$base_url/bookmarks\" 401\n      fi\n      ;;\n  esac\n\n  # Stop server\n  kill $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true\n  wait $server_pid 2>/dev/null || true\n\n  # Write results\n  write_functional_json \"$outdir/functional.json\" \"${results[@]}\"\n}\n\n# Helper: run a single endpoint check\nrun_check() {\n  local -n arr=$1; shift\n  local name=\"$1\"; shift\n  local method=\"$1\"; shift\n  local url=\"$1\"; shift\n  local expected_status=\"$1\"; shift\n\n  local extra_args=()\n  local body_contains=\"\"\n  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do\n    case $1 in\n      --data) extra_args+=(-H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --header) extra_args+=(-H \"$2\"); shift 2 ;;\n      --body-contains) body_contains=\"$2\"; shift 2 ;;\n      *) shift ;;\n    esac\n  done\n\n  local response_code response_body\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

body.description\n        : null;\n  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();\n\n  db.prepare(\n    `\n      UPDATE bookmarks\n      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, updated_at = ?\n      WHERE id = ?\n    `,\n  ).run(url, title, description, updatedAt, id);\n\n  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id));\n});\n\nrouter.delete(\"/:id\", (req, res) => {\n  const id = parseId(req.params.id);\n  const result =\n    id === null\n      ? { changes: 0 }\n      : db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?\").run(id);\n\n  if (result.changes === 0) {\n    res.status(404).json({ error: \"bookmark not found\" });\n    return;\n  }\n\n  res.status(204).send();\n});\n\nexport default router;\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T6-pagination/events.jsonl:49:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_24","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' docs/product/api.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Authentication

Users are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are
stored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer
token after authentication.

### `POST /auth/register`

Creates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.

### `POST /auth/login`

Authenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "token": "<jwt>" }
```

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one
of that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.

All bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing, empty, whitespace-only,
or when `url` is not a valid URL.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered
by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is provided as an empty or
whitespace-only string, or when `url` is not a valid URL.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned
by the authenticated user.

All folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing, empty, or whitespace-only.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by
`id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the
authenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` when provided and refreshes `updated_at` when the
name changes.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is provided as an empty or whitespace-only
string.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:10:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

## License

MIT

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:25:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_10","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,320p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { db } from "./db";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return undefined;
  }

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value === "number") {
    return Number.isInteger(value) && value > 0 ? value : undefined;
  }

  if (typeof value === "string" && /^\d+$/.test(value)) {
    const id = Number(value);
    return id > 0 ? id : undefined;
  }

  return undefined;
}

function findBookmark(id: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

function folderExists(id: number): boolean {
  const row = db.prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ?").get(id);
  return row !== undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required and must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  const folderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

  if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  if (typeof folderId === "number" && !folderExists(folderId)) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(
      body.url.trim(),
      body.title.trim(),
      description,
      folderId ?? null,
      now,
      now,
    );

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid));
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  let url = existing.url;
  let title = existing.title;
  let description = existing.description;
  let folderId = existing.folder_id;

  if (body.url !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be valid" });
      return;
    }

    url = body.url.trim();
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    title = body.title.trim();
  }

  if (body.description !== undefined) {
    description = typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  }

  if (body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    const parsedFolderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

    if (parsedFolderId === undefined) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    if (typeof parsedFolderId === "number" && !folderExists(parsedFolderId)) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    folderId = parsedFolderId;
  }

  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ?").run(id);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ?`)
    .get(id) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid)));
});

router.get("/", (_req, res) => {
  const folders = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all() as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number) => {
    db.prepare(
      "UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ? WHERE folder_id = ?",
    ).run(new Date().toISOString(), folderId);
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ?").run(folderId);
  });

  deleteFolder(id);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:30:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_13","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' src/db.ts"","aggregated_output":"import Database from "better-sqlite3";

export const db = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

export function initializeDatabase(): void {
  db.exec(`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      url TEXT NOT NULL,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      folder_id INTEGER,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id)
    )
  `);

  const columns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!columns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)",
    );
  }
}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:43:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_21","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' docs/stories/T3-folder-support.md"","aggregated_output":"# T3 Folder Support

## Status

implemented

## Lane

normal

## Product Contract

The API exposes CRUD operations for folders. Bookmarks can optionally reference
a folder through nullable `folder_id`; deleting a folder leaves its bookmarks in
place and clears their `folder_id`.

## Relevant Product Docs

- `docs/product/api.md`
- `PRODUCT_SPEC.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- `POST /folders` creates a folder with required `name`.
- `GET /folders` returns an array of folders.
- `GET /folders/:id` returns a folder with its bookmarks or HTTP `404`.
- `PUT /folders/:id` updates a folder name or returns HTTP `404`.
- `DELETE /folders/:id` deletes a folder with HTTP `204`, returns HTTP `404`
  when missing, and nullifies assigned bookmarks.
- `POST /bookmarks` and `PUT /bookmarks/:id` accept optional `folder_id`.
- Non-existent `folder_id` values return HTTP `400`.
- Existing bookmark CRUD endpoints continue to work.

## Design Notes

- Commands: create, update, and delete folder rows; assign and unassign bookmark
  `folder_id`.
- Queries: list folders, get a folder with its bookmarks, and existing bookmark
  reads include `folder_id`.
- API: `/folders` collection and `/folders/:id` item routes.
- Tables: `folders`; `bookmarks.folder_id`.
- Domain rules: folder `name` is required; bookmark `folder_id` must reference
  an existing folder when provided.
- UI surfaces: none.

## Validation

| Layer | Expected proof |
| --- | --- |
| Unit | TypeScript build succeeds. |
| Integration | Curl checks cover folder CRUD, folder bookmark membership, folder deletion nullification, invalid `folder_id`, and bookmark regression. |
| E2E | Not applicable for API-only story. |
| Platform | SQLite `data.db` migrates existing `bookmarks` tables with nullable `folder_id`. |
| Release | Not applicable for benchmark task. |

## Harness Delta

No harness process changes were needed.

## Evidence

- Implementation changed `src/db.ts`, `src/index.ts`, `src/bookmarks.ts`, and
  `src/folders.ts`.
- Product docs changed `docs/product/api.md`.
- `npm run build` could not run because dependencies are not installed in this
  sandbox (`tsc: command not found`).
- `npm install --offline --prefer-offline --no-audit --no-fund` failed because
  `@types/bcryptjs` was not present in the local npm cache.

# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks may optionally belong to a folder through nullable `folder_id`.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of all bookmarks ordered by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db`.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of all folders ordered by `id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing bookmarks
assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.
docs/product/README.md
# Product Docs

This directory is intentionally generic and mostly empty in Harness v0.

When a user provides a project spec, derive smaller product contract files here
instead of keeping one large spec as the living plan. Name files by the product
domains that actually exist in that spec, for example `overview.md`,
`billing.md`, `workflows.md`, `permissions.md`, or `api-conventions.md`.

Do not create domain files before the spec just to fill the folder. Empty
structure is healthier than fake product truth.

## Update Rule

When behavior changes:

1. Update the affected product doc.
2. Update or create the story packet.
3. Update durable proof status with `scripts/harness story add` or
   `scripts/harness story update`.
4. Record a decision if the change affects architecture, scope, risk, or a
   previously settled product rule.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:89:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_46","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,300p' src/folders.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { requireAuth } from "./auth";
import { db } from "./db";

type Folder = {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type FolderBody = {
  name?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const folderSelect = "id, name, created_at, updated_at";
const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

router.use(requireAuth);

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function findFolder(id: number, userId: number): Folder | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`)
    .get(id, userId) as Folder | undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO folders (name, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(body.name.trim(), userId, now, now);

  res.status(201).json(findFolder(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId));
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const folders = db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${folderSelect} FROM folders WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`)
    .all(userId) as Folder[];

  res.status(200).json(folders);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const folder = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!folder || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(id, userId) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json({ ...folder, bookmarks });
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as FolderBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.name)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "name must be non-empty" });
    return;
  }

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE folders
      SET name = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
    `,
  ).run(body.name.trim(), new Date().toISOString(), id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(findFolder(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findFolder(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "folder not found" });
    return;
  }

  const deleteFolder = db.transaction((folderId: number, ownerId: number) => {
    db.prepare(
      "UPDATE bookmarks SET folder_id = NULL, updated_at = ? WHERE folder_id = ? AND user_id = ?",
    ).run(new Date().toISOString(), folderId, ownerId);
    db.prepare("DELETE FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(
      folderId,
      ownerId,
    );
  });

  deleteFolder(id, userId);
  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:91:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_47","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,320p' src/bookmarks.ts"","aggregated_output":"import { Router } from "express";
import { requireAuth } from "./auth";
import { db } from "./db";

type Bookmark = {
  id: number;
  url: string;
  title: string;
  description: string | null;
  folder_id: number | null;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
};

type BookmarkBody = {
  url?: unknown;
  title?: unknown;
  description?: unknown;
  folder_id?: unknown;
};

const router = Router();

const bookmarkSelect =
  "id, url, title, description, folder_id, created_at, updated_at";

router.use(requireAuth);

function isNonEmptyString(value: unknown): value is string {
  return typeof value === "string" && value.trim().length > 0;
}

function isValidUrl(value: string): boolean {
  try {
    new URL(value);
    return true;
  } catch {
    return false;
  }
}

function parseId(value: string): number | null {
  const id = Number(value);
  return Number.isInteger(id) && id > 0 ? id : null;
}

function parseFolderId(value: unknown): number | null | undefined {
  if (value === undefined) {
    return undefined;
  }

  if (value === null) {
    return null;
  }

  if (typeof value === "number") {
    return Number.isInteger(value) && value > 0 ? value : undefined;
  }

  if (typeof value === "string" && /^\d+$/.test(value)) {
    const id = Number(value);
    return id > 0 ? id : undefined;
  }

  return undefined;
}

function findBookmark(id: number, userId: number): Bookmark | undefined {
  return db
    .prepare(`SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?`)
    .get(id, userId) as Bookmark | undefined;
}

function folderExists(id: number, userId: number): boolean {
  const row = db
    .prepare("SELECT id FROM folders WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?")
    .get(id, userId);
  return row !== undefined;
}

router.post("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "url is required and must be valid" });
    return;
  }

  if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: "title is required" });
    return;
  }

  const now = new Date().toISOString();
  const description =
    typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  const folderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

  if (folderId === undefined && body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  if (typeof folderId === "number" && !folderExists(folderId, userId)) {
    res
      .status(400)
      .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
    return;
  }

  const result = db
    .prepare(
      `
        INSERT INTO bookmarks (url, title, description, folder_id, user_id, created_at, updated_at)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
      `,
    )
    .run(
      body.url.trim(),
      body.title.trim(),
      description,
      folderId ?? null,
      userId,
      now,
      now,
    );

  const bookmark = findBookmark(Number(result.lastInsertRowid), userId);
  res.status(201).json(bookmark);
});

router.get("/", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const bookmarks = db
    .prepare(
      `SELECT ${bookmarkSelect} FROM bookmarks WHERE user_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC`,
    )
    .all(userId) as Bookmark[];

  res.status(200).json(bookmarks);
});

router.get("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const bookmark = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!bookmark) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(200).json(bookmark);
});

router.put("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const existing = id === null ? undefined : findBookmark(id, userId);

  if (!existing || id === null) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  const body = req.body as BookmarkBody;
  let url = existing.url;
  let title = existing.title;
  let description = existing.description;
  let folderId = existing.folder_id;

  if (body.url !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.url) || !isValidUrl(body.url.trim())) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "url must be valid" });
      return;
    }

    url = body.url.trim();
  }

  if (body.title !== undefined) {
    if (!isNonEmptyString(body.title)) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: "title must be non-empty" });
      return;
    }

    title = body.title.trim();
  }

  if (body.description !== undefined) {
    description = typeof body.description === "string" ? body.description : null;
  }

  if (body.folder_id !== undefined) {
    const parsedFolderId = parseFolderId(body.folder_id);

    if (parsedFolderId === undefined) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    if (
      typeof parsedFolderId === "number" &&
      !folderExists(parsedFolderId, userId)
    ) {
      res
        .status(400)
        .json({ error: "folder_id must reference an existing folder" });
      return;
    }

    folderId = parsedFolderId;
  }

  const updatedAt = new Date().toISOString();

  db.prepare(
    `
      UPDATE bookmarks
      SET url = ?, title = ?, description = ?, folder_id = ?, updated_at = ?
      WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?
    `,
  ).run(url, title, description, folderId, updatedAt, id, userId);

  res.status(200).json(findBookmark(id, userId));
});

router.delete("/:id", (req, res) => {
  const userId = req.userId as number;
  const id = parseId(req.params.id);
  const result =
    id === null
      ? { changes: 0 }
      : db.prepare("DELETE FROM bookmarks WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?").run(id, userId);

  if (result.changes === 0) {
    res.status(404).json({ error: "bookmark not found" });
    return;
  }

  res.status(204).send();
});

export default router;

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:93:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_48","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' src/db.ts"","aggregated_output":"import Database from "better-sqlite3";

export const db = new Database("data.db");

db.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
db.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");

export function initializeDatabase(): void {
  db.exec(`
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      email TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
      password_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS folders (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      name TEXT NOT NULL,
      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
    );

    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bookmarks (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      url TEXT NOT NULL,
      title TEXT NOT NULL,
      description TEXT,
      folder_id INTEGER,
      user_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
      FOREIGN KEY (folder_id) REFERENCES folders(id),
      FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
    )
  `);

  const bookmarkColumns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(bookmarks)").all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "folder_id")) {
    db.exec(
      "ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN folder_id INTEGER REFERENCES folders(id)",
    );
  }

  if (!bookmarkColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
    db.exec("ALTER TABLE bookmarks ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)");
  }

  const folderColumns = db.prepare("PRAGMA table_info(folders)").all() as Array<{
    name: string;
  }>;

  if (!folderColumns.some((column) => column.name === "user_id")) {
    db.exec("ALTER TABLE folders ADD COLUMN user_id INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)");
  }
}

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T4-authentication/events.jsonl:133:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_68","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' docs/product/api.md"","aggregated_output":"# Bookmark Manager API

## Runtime

- The API runs as a Node.js 20+ TypeScript service using Express.
- The development server listens on port `3000` unless `PORT` is provided.
- Application data is stored in SQLite at `data.db`.

## Health

### `GET /health`

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "status": "ok" }
```

## Authentication

Users are persisted in the `users` SQLite table in `data.db`. Passwords are
stored as bcrypt hashes. Bookmark and folder endpoints require a JWT bearer
token after authentication.

### `POST /auth/register`

Creates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created user without `password_hash`.

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `409` when the email is already registered.

### `POST /auth/login`

Authenticates a user account. The JSON body must include a valid `email` and a
`password` with at least 8 characters.

Returns HTTP `200` with:

```json
{ "token": "<jwt>" }
```

Returns HTTP `400` when email or password validation fails.
Returns HTTP `401` when credentials are wrong.

## Bookmarks

Bookmarks are persisted in the `bookmarks` SQLite table in `data.db`.
Bookmarks are owned by the authenticated user and may optionally belong to one
of that user's folders through nullable `folder_id`.

All bookmark endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /bookmarks`

Creates a bookmark. The JSON body must include non-empty `url` and `title`
strings. `description` and `folder_id` are optional and may be omitted.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created bookmark.

Returns HTTP `400` when `url` or `title` is missing or empty.
Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `GET /bookmarks`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's bookmarks ordered
by `id`.

### `GET /bookmarks/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the bookmark when it exists.

Returns HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for `id`.

### `PUT /bookmarks/:id`

Updates `url`, `title`, `description`, and/or `folder_id` for an existing
bookmark and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated bookmark, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark
exists for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `folder_id` is provided and does not reference an
existing folder owned by the authenticated user.

### `DELETE /bookmarks/:id`

Deletes a bookmark.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no bookmark exists for
`id`.

## Folders

Folders are persisted in the `folders` SQLite table in `data.db` and are owned
by the authenticated user.

All folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and return HTTP
`401` when the token is missing or invalid.

### `POST /folders`

Creates a folder. The JSON body must include a non-empty `name` string.

Returns HTTP `201` with the created folder.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `GET /folders`

Returns HTTP `200` with an array of the authenticated user's folders ordered by
`id`.

### `GET /folders/:id`

Returns HTTP `200` with the folder and a `bookmarks` array containing the
authenticated user's bookmarks assigned to the folder.

Returns HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

### `PUT /folders/:id`

Updates the folder `name` and refreshes `updated_at`.

Returns HTTP `200` with the updated folder, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists
for `id`.

Returns HTTP `400` when `name` is missing or empty.

### `DELETE /folders/:id`

Deletes a folder. Bookmarks assigned to the deleted folder remain in the
`bookmarks` table and have `folder_id` set to `null`.

Returns HTTP `204` when deleted, or HTTP `404` when no folder exists for `id`.

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:5:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_1","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

MIT

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T1-project-setup/events.jsonl:26:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_12","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:12:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_4","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' README.md"","aggregated_output":"# Harness Benchmark

A controlled benchmark for measuring the effectiveness of [harness-experimental](https://github.com/hoangnb24/harness-experimental) across development phases.

## What This Is

A pre-seeded TypeScript/Express project with **6 fixed tasks** (T1–T6) that an AI agent executes. The benchmark measures time, token cost, code quality, and harness compliance. By re-running after each harness phase, we objectively measure whether the harness improved agent productivity.

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

```bash
# Codex CLI (primary agent)
npm install -g @openai/codex
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."

# System dependencies
node --version  # v20+
jq --version    # for JSON parsing
sqlite3 --version  # for harness compliance checks
```

### Run the Benchmark

```bash
# Baseline run (against harness main branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness main --run-id baseline

# Phase 2 run (against feature branch)
./benchmark/run.sh --agent codex --harness phase-2/observability-taxonomy --run-id phase-2

# Compare results
./benchmark/compare.sh baseline phase-2
```

### View Results

```bash
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/report.md     # Human-readable summary
cat benchmark/runs/<run-id>/scores.json   # Machine-readable scores
```

## Project Structure

```
harness-benchmark/
├── README.md                 # This file
├── PRODUCT_SPEC.md           # Product specification (what the agent builds)
├── package.json              # Pre-seeded dependencies (Express, better-sqlite3, vitest)
├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript configuration
├── src/
│   └── index.ts              # Empty entrypoint (agent builds from here)
├── benchmark/
│   ├── PROTOCOL.md           # How runs work, what's measured, rules
│   ├── run.sh                # Main orchestrator script
│   ├── compare.sh            # Compare two run results
│   ├── tasks/                # Task prompts (T1-T6.md)
│   ├── rubrics/              # Objective scoring checklists
│   ├── lib/                  # Runner helper scripts
│   │   ├── prepare.sh        # Harness installation
│   │   ├── invoke.sh         # Agent invocation + telemetry
│   │   ├── check-functional.sh
│   │   ├── check-harness.sh
│   │   ├── check-quality.sh
│   │   └── report.sh
│   ├── runs/                 # Output directory (git-tracked results)
│   │   └── .gitkeep
│   └── seeds/                # Checkpoint states for partial re-runs
│       └── .gitkeep
```

## The Benchmark Cycle

1. **Tag** benchmark repo → `benchmark-v1`
2. **Install harness** from target ref (main, feature branch)
3. **Run T1–T6** sequentially via Codex CLI
4. **Score** against objective rubrics
5. **Compare** to previous runs

Each phase must **earn its merge** by moving the numbers.

## Tasks Overview

| Task | Name | Risk Lane | What It Tests |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|
| T1 | Project Setup | tiny | Basic scaffolding, health endpoint |
| T2 | CRUD Bookmarks | normal | Core API implementation |
| T3 | Folder Support | normal | Feature addition on existing code |
| T4 | Authentication | high-risk | Complex feature with security implications |
| T5 | Bug Fix | normal | Diagnosis and targeted fix |
| T6 | Pagination | normal | Refactoring existing API responses |

## Metrics

| Metric | What It Measures | Source |
|--------|-----------------|--------|
| Wall time | Speed of completion | `timing.json` |
| Token cost | API cost efficiency | `tokens.json` (from Codex JSONL) |
| Functional score | Does the code work? | `check-functional.sh` (curl tests) |
| Harness compliance | Did the agent use the harness? | `check-harness.sh` (sqlite3 queries) |
| Trace quality | How detailed are the traces? | `check-quality.sh` |
| Lane accuracy | Correct risk classification? | `lane.json` |

benchmark/runs/baseline-main/T2-crud-bookmarks/events.jsonl:20:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_9","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,260p' PRODUCT_SPEC.md"","aggregated_output":"# Product Specification: Bookmark Manager API

## Overview

A REST API for managing bookmarks with folder organization, user authentication, and pagination. Built with TypeScript, Express, and better-sqlite3.

## Functional Requirements

### Core Entities

- **Bookmark**: A saved URL with title, optional description, timestamps
- **Folder**: A named collection of bookmarks
- **User**: An authenticated account that owns bookmarks and folders

### API Endpoints

#### Health
- `GET /health` → `{ "status": "ok" }`

#### Bookmarks (after auth: scoped to authenticated user)
- `POST /bookmarks` — Create a bookmark (requires: url, title)
- `GET /bookmarks` — List all bookmarks (paginated after T6)
- `GET /bookmarks/:id` — Get single bookmark
- `PUT /bookmarks/:id` — Update a bookmark
- `DELETE /bookmarks/:id` — Delete a bookmark

#### Folders
- `POST /folders` — Create a folder (requires: name)
- `GET /folders` — List all folders
- `GET /folders/:id` — Get folder with its bookmarks
- `PUT /folders/:id` — Update folder name
- `DELETE /folders/:id` — Delete folder (bookmarks become unfoldered)

#### Authentication
- `POST /auth/register` — Create account (email + password)
- `POST /auth/login` — Get JWT token
- All bookmark/folder endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>` after auth is implemented

### Validation Rules

- Bookmark `url` must be a valid URL
- Bookmark `title` must be non-empty string
- Folder `name` must be non-empty string
- Email must be valid format
- Password must be >= 8 characters

### Pagination (after T6)

- `GET /bookmarks?page=1&limit=20`
- Response shape: `{ data: [...], page: 1, limit: 20, total: 45 }`
- Default: page=1, limit=20
- Max limit: 100

## Technical Stack (Pre-decided)

- **Runtime**: Node.js 20+
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Framework**: Express
- **Database**: SQLite via better-sqlite3
- **Testing**: Vitest
- **Port**: 3000

## Non-Functional Requirements

- All responses are JSON
- Proper HTTP status codes (200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 404, 500)
- Passwords stored as hashes (bcrypt or similar)
- JWT tokens for session management
- Database file: `data.db` (application data)

## Functional Checks (automated)

| # | Check | Method | Pass Criteria |

| 1 | Create bookmark | `POST /bookmarks {"url":"https://example.com","title":"Test"}` | Status 201 |
| 2 | List bookmarks | `GET /bookmarks` | Status 200 + array |
